text: Writing clean code is essential for any programmer. The main goal is to make your code readable, so that other developers—or your future self—can understand it easily. A key practice is following naming conventions: variable and function names should clearly describe their purpose. For example, use "calculateTotalPrice" instead of "calc" or "x". Writing short, focused functions that do only one thing also improves maintainability. When each function has a single responsibility, debugging and updating the code becomes much simpler. Ultimately, clean code saves time and reduces errors in the long run.
